source.videoTitle: 22 Things You Need To Stop Doing On Your Disney World Trip!

tips.text: If your child cannot see during fireworks, move further back, watch from an angle, or use a less crowded viewing area instead of putting them on shoulders.

source.videoTitle: 22 Things You Need To Stop Doing On Your Disney World Trip!

tips.text: If you need to check your phone, regroup your family, or reapply sunscreen, move to the side of walkways instead of stopping in the middle of pedestrian traffic.

source.videoTitle: 22 Things You Need To Stop Doing On Your Disney World Trip!

tips.text: Do not cut in line by having one person hold a spot for a large group that arrives later, as this is considered line-cutting and creates tension.

source.videoTitle: 22 Things You Need To Stop Doing On Your Disney World Trip!

tips.text: Pace yourself during your trip by taking breaks, staying hydrated, and not trying to maximize every single second to avoid exhaustion.

source.videoTitle: 22 Things You Need To Stop Doing On Your Disney World Trip!

tips.text: Learn to use the Disney World app before your trip by practicing with mobile ordering, wait times, maps, and Lightning Lane booking.

source.videoTitle: 22 Things You Need To Stop Doing On Your Disney World Trip!

tips.text: Avoid broadcasting your entire experience or spreading misinformation in queues and on transportation, as it creates a more exhausting environment for others.

source.videoTitle: 22 Things You Need To Stop Doing On Your Disney World Trip!

tips.text: Give people adequate personal space in queues, and do not crawl up their backs or repeatedly bump into them.

source.videoTitle: 22 Things You Need To Stop Doing On Your Disney World Trip!

tips.text: Bring portable snacks like pretzels, protein bars, or fruit snacks and have them delivered to your hotel to save money and avoid emergency purchases in the parks.

source.videoTitle: 22 Things You Need To Stop Doing On Your Disney World Trip!

tips.text: Maintain active visual confirmation of children at all times, especially in crowded and distracting environments like parades and fireworks exits.

source.videoTitle: 22 Things You Need To Stop Doing On Your Disney World Trip!

tips.text: Do not pressure cast members for 'magical moments' or special treatment; genuine magic happens through calm, kind, and normal interactions.

source.videoTitle: 22 Things You Need To Stop Doing On Your Disney World Trip!

tips.text: Use the restroom before entering long queues or crowded areas like transportation loading zones to avoid the stress and difficulty of leaving mid-experience.

source.videoTitle: 22 Things You Need To Stop Doing On Your Disney World Trip!

tips.text: If you want to ride The Land pavilion attractions, check the wait times early as they can increase significantly throughout the day.
